title: From space rovers to hunger maps: How AI is reshaping humanitarian aid
description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is often discussed for its potential threats to humanity. But humanitarian organisations are using it to predict hunger, map destruction and deliver aid without sending people into danger.
content:
Delivering food through conflict zones, minefields and floods can put humanitarian workers at mortal risk.
Now, technology developed to control rovers on distant planets is being adapted to take aid workers out of some of the world’s most dangerous a... [3746 chars]

title: AI, science and the risks in China’s reliance on imported precision equipment
description: The country is dependent on overseas high-end scientific instruments, crimping use of artificial intelligence, researcher says
content:
China’s reliance on imports for the most sophisticated scientific instruments could hold back the country’s use of AI in science, according to a leading Chinese researcher.
Advanced equipment such as mass spectrometers was essential for generating th... [4589 chars]

title: Simaero plans to add more simulators, tap local engg talent in India: CEO
description: French firm Simaero will invest 200 million euros in India over ten years. The company plans to expand its aviation training facility in Gurugram. It aims to develop local engineering talent for simulator technology services. Simaero expects to operate four to six simulators by 2029. Artificial intelligence will enhance operational efficiency and learning experiences.
content:

title: Tata Consultancy Services plans up to 8,900 AI deployment engineers, seeks AI acquisitions
description: Tata Consultancy Services is building a large team of forward-deployed engineers. The company is also actively seeking artificial intelligence acquisitions. This strategy aims to create new business opportunities rather than disrupt outsourcing. TCS believes deep client knowledge is key to integrating and deploying AI systems. The firm is investing significantly in talent development and AI accessibility.

title: India's Tata Consultancy Services plans up to 8,900 AI deployment engineers, seeks AI acquisitions
description: Tata Consultancy Services is building a team of up to 8,900 forward-deployed engineers ‌and hunting for AI acquisitions as it bets artificial intelligence will create new business rather than undermine outsourcing, two TCS executives told Reuters.
content:
The strategy emerges amid investor concern that AI could disrupt India's $315 billion IT services industry by reducing demand for engineering teams, ​shortening project timelines and squeezing prices as clients seek a share of productivity gains.

title: LTM Profit Jumps 17% to ₹1,468 Cr in Apr
description: IT services firm LTM, promoted by Larsen & Toubro, reported a 17.1% year-on-year jump in its June quarter net profit to Rs 1,468.6 crore in Bengaluru. Driven by an artificial intelligence-centric strategy, the company's overall revenue grew 18% to Rs 11,608 crore, despite facing geographic headwinds in Europe and the Rest of World.
content:
IT Services company LTM on Saturday reported a 17.1% jump in its June quarter net profit to ₹1,468.6 crore compared to the year-ago period.
The company, promoted by EPC major Larsen & Toubro, had reported a net profit of ₹1,254.6 crore in the year-ag... [1434 chars]
